records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Discontinued (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Discontinuing.]

defs: To interrupt the continuance of; to intermit, as a practice or habit; to put an end to; to cause to cease; to cease using, to stop; to leave off.
quotes: Set up their conventicles again, which had been discontinued.
I have discontinued school Above a twelvemonth.
Taught the Greek tongue, discontinued before in these parts the space of seven hundred years.
They modify and discriminate the voice, without appearing to discontinue it.

defs: To lose continuity or cohesion of parts; to be disrupted or broken off.
To be separated or severed; to part.
quotes: Thyself shalt discontinue from thine heritage.
